experiments.reasoning: The passage describes a deliberate procedure involving systematic observations of a phenomenon (the luminous appearance in a pullet) under controlled conditions (darkened chamber) to investigate its cause and compare it to another observed phenomenon (the luminousness of veal). The author explicitly mentions sending meat into the larder and observing its behavior over time, which aligns with the definition of an experiment.
experiments.description: The experiment involved observing a pullet (young hen) that exhibited a light source in its larder. The author moved it into a darkened chamber to further investigate the luminous spots, particularly those near the rump and at the tip of the rump. The goal was to determine whether this phenomenon was unique to the specific veal or if it could be generalized to other meats, especially in the context of putrefaction or freshness.
experiments.quote: having several nights sent purpoely into the Larder, to observe, whether any Veal, since brought thither, or any other meat, did afford any light, a negative answer was alwaies brought me back; save at one time, which happened to be within less than 48 hours of that, at which the Luminousness of the Veal had been first taken notice of; for at this time there was in the same Larder a conspicuous light seen in a Pullet, that hung up there, which having caus’d to be brought up into a darkned place in my chamber in the night time, I perceived four or five luminous places, which were not indeed near so large as those of the Veal, but were little less vivid than they. All of these I took notice to be either upon or near the Rump, and that, which appear’d most like a Spark of Fire, shone at the very tip of that part.
experiments.outcome: The luminous spots in the pullet were observed in a darkened chamber, and the author noted that the meat was fresh and not foul or ill-sent. The experiment suggests that the luminousness was not due to putrefaction or decay but rather a transient phenomenon tied to the specific conditions of the meat’s storage and exposure.
experiments.location: Larder (mentioned in the text) and a darkened chamber in the author's chamber.
